title: Rebuilt Name Changes
wikitext:
[]: Rebuilt Name Changes             Quote Post by Stan » Wed Nov 14, 2012 8:56 pm I don't know if this question was addressed on the forums here or not and I couldn't find the forum post if it was, so I'll ask again [sorry if I'm re-asking what someone already answered but I'm new here]. Asuka Langley Soryu from NGE had a last name change to Shikinami in the rebuilt of eva films. I was just wondering why they did it and for what purpose? I never really wondered why, but tonight I suddenly got the random urge to find out aha~ Was it done by Anno to just refresh the films and make them stand out even more from his original work of NGE, or was there a reason of some sort? Since the 3.0 film is coming out, I kinda had a thought that Kaworu's last name was not yet confirmed or even mentioned to be the same as it used to be. It would be kinda funny if the 3.0 movie reveals they also did a random last name change for him, but thats just me going off topic. So yeah, what is the reson behind Asukas name change?

[]: Re: Rebuilt Name Changes             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Nov 14, 2012 9:17 pm Stan wrote: Since the 3.0 film is coming out, I kinda had a thought that Kaworu's last name was not yet confirmed or even mentioned to be the same as it used to be.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Nov 15, 2012 2:11 am We've not had an explanation of why Asuka's name change, just statements to the effect that there's a reason for it. There's at least one Sohryu vs Shikinami thread which covers all we know.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:42 am Honestly I just figured they wanted the theme of all the female pilots having names that end in Nami.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:48 am Darkwing wrote: Honestly I just figured they wanted the theme of all the female pilots having names that end in Nami.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:49 am Darkwing wrote: Honestly I just figured they wanted the theme of all the female pilots having names that end in Nami.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:56 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: It's pretty common for IJN destroyers, all the Ayanami class destroyers ended in "nami", which means "wave".

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Nov 16, 2012 12:01 pm riffraff11235 wrote: Right! I keep forgetting about Anno's status as a military Otaku. I guess he just changed the name for consistency, then.
